name: Crumblin'
recipeInstructions:
1. Crumblin' is a traditional Texas dish/drink characteristic of the Texan's penchant for "waste not, want not."
2. In the early days of Texas statehood, cornmeal flour was more readily available than wheat flour.
3. Consequently, corn bread was a prevalent dish in most households.
4. There was always leftover corn bread, and since most Texans had a family cow, sweet milk from the cow was mixed with the bread into an interesting "drink."
5. To make crumblin', pour 2/3 cup of sweet milk into a large glass.
6. Crumble fresh-made or day-old cornbread into the glass of milk.
7. Let soak to desired consistency and drink.
8. (Optional) Add a tablespoon of finely minced sweet white onions for variation.
